name: com.alexanderlarsen.saneject
author: Alexander Larsen
description:
Inject dependencies in the Unity Editor, not Play Mode, by writing them directly into serialized fields at edit-time using familiar DI APIs, so everything stays visible in the Inspector, including interfaces.

No runtime container. No startup cost. No hidden wiring. No weird lifecycles. Just simple, deterministic edit-time DI that works with Unity, not around it.

samples.description:
A small Unity game demonstrating how Saneject is used in a real project.

Explore scene, prefab, and object-level scopes, global registrations, and runtime proxy bindings working together in a multi-scene setup. Gameplay is intentionally simple so you can focus on architecture: systems communicate through interfaces, UI and prefabs resolve scene services via proxies, and the game loop is wired without direct references.

Includes bootstrap flow, additive scene loading, UI integration, and end-to-end dependency wiring. To run: add StartScene, GameScene, UIScene to Build Settings (in order), open StartScene, and press Play.
